channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Supreme Court doesn't seem convinced FDA was unfair in blocking flavored vapes teen use increased
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A majority of Supreme Court justices didn't seem convinced Monday that federal regulators misled companies before refusing to allow them to sell sweet-flavored vaping products following a surge in teen e-cigarette use.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An ex-detective accused of abusing women died in an apparent suicide as his trial was starting
item.description: TOPEKA, Kan. (AP) — A white ex-police detective in Kansas died Monday in an apparent suicide just before the start of his criminal trial over allegations that he sexually assaulted Black women and terrorized those who tried fight back.
item.dc:creator: JOHN HANNA, HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH and NICK INGRAM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Wave of Democrats slam Biden's pardon of his son Hunter
item.description: While some Democrats extended a measure of sympathy, President Biden has taken heat on several fronts.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KRON4

item.title: Harvey Weinstein hospitalized after 'alarming blood test,' attorney says
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Harvey Weinstein was hospitalized Monday following an “alarming blood test,” his attorney said, less than a week after the disgraced movie mogul filed a legal claim alleging substandard medical care at New York City's notorious jail complex.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Coast Guard suspends its search for the crew of a capsized fishing boat in the Gulf of Alaska
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — The search for five people believed to be aboard a fishing vessel that capsized in rough seas in the Gulf of Alaska has been suspended, the Coast Guard said Monday. The search lasted nearly a day and covered more than 108 square nautical miles (370 square kilometers).
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: White House unveils 'A Season of Peace and Light' Christmas decorations
item.description: First Lady Jill Biden has decked out the White House with some whimsical decorations to evoke the “peace and light” of the season.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump warns of 'hell to pay' if Hamas doesn't release hostages
item.description: President-elect Trump on Monday threatened to unleash “hell” in the Middle East if hostages held by Hamas in the Gaza Strip are not released before his inauguration Jan.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9

item.title: Prosecutor says officers acted 'recklessly' in Black man's death during mental health crisis
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Two Indianapolis police officers charged in the death of a Black man — who was shocked with a Taser during a mental health crisis — acted “recklessly" by restraining him face down longer than necessary, a prosecutor said during opening statements Monday.
item.dc:creator: RICK CALLAHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man arrested for allegedly trying to pimp Denver women
item.description: A Los Angeles man was arrested for allegedly trying to pimp two Denver women, and prosecutors are looking for more victims in the area.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, NewsNation

item.title: Man recovering after assault over McDonald's sandwich
item.description: "Slammed the window in my face, then told me to come back with a better attitude," Karrigan Norwood said.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Beachy, NewsNation

item.title: White House celebrates 'peace and light' with holiday décor
item.description: The idea behind the theme, according to the White House, is to encourage the more than 100,000 visitors who pass through the doors at 1600 Pennsylvania Ave. during Christmastime to "embrace the peace and light of the holiday season.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, WGN 9

item.title: Cyber Monday shoppers expected to set a record on the year's biggest day for online shopping
item.description: Consumers in the United States are scouring the internet for online deals as they look to take advantage of the post-Thanksgiving shopping marathon with Cyber Monday.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Fugitive, 19, sought in Texas accused of killing 70-year-old man in Georgia carjacking
item.description: A man wanted in Harris County, Texas, was arrested and charged after an alleged carjacking and killing of a 70-year-old man in Atlanta, Georgia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden displays AIDS Memorial Quilt at White House to observe World AIDS Day
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden on Sunday displayed the AIDS Memorial Quilt on the White House South Lawn for the first time in observance of World AIDS Day.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Girl Scout saves dad days after learning CPR with her troop
item.description: Just days before Vada Carawan's father suffered a heart attack, the 11-year-old and her Girl Scout troop had learned CPR.
item.dc:creator: Deja Parker, ABC 4

item.title: Lou Carnesecca, Hall of Fame coach who led St. John's for 24 seasons, dies
item.description: The excitable St. John’s coach whose outlandish sweaters became an emblem of his team’s rousing Final Four run in 1985, died at 99 on Saturday, just a few weeks shy of his 100th birthday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: President Biden gives remarks for World AIDS Day
item.description: The White House is holding an event Sunday to commemorate World AIDS Day with survivors, their families, and advocates.
item.dc:creator: Mike Costanza, KRON4

item.title: Heavy snow and numbing temperatures keep parts of the US in a deep freeze
item.description: Heavy snowfall and numbing temperatures kept parts of the U.S. in a deep freeze Sunday as the Thanksgiving holiday weekend draws to a close.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What the cost-of-living adjustment increase in Social Security means
item.description: A 2.5% cost-of-living adjustment increase will mean an extra $50 a month for most Social Security and SSI recipients.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, KRON4

item.title: Naughty or nice? 24 holiday coffee drinks ranked from 'healthiest' to unhealthiest
item.description: The holiday season is the perfect time of year to enjoy a cup of coffee, whether you're settling into a coffee shop armchair and catching up with friends, or pulling into a drive-thru for a quick reprieve while running errands.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Ward, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Southwest Airlines to end cabin service earlier on flights to reduce chance of injury
item.description: Southwest Airlines says it is ending cabin service earlier on flights, requiring passengers to do the usual pre-landing procedures such as ensuring their seatbelts are fastened and returning their seats to an upright position earlier than before. Beginning on Dec.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bob Bryar, former My Chemical Romance drummer, dies at 44
item.description: (WGHP) — Bob Bryar, the drummer of the rock band My Chemical Romance from 2004 to 2014 has died, TMZ reports. He was 44. According to TMZ, Bryar was found dead in his Tennessee home Tuesday after last being seen alive on November 4.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, Fox 5

item.title: Truck carrying 44,000 pounds of dried chickpeas catches fire in Death Valley National Park
item.description: A truck carrying 44,000 pounds of dried chickpeas caught fire in Death Valley National Park, according to the National Park Service.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, Fox 5

item.title: Trump wants pardoned real estate developer Charles Kushner to become US ambassador to France
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump said Saturday he intends to nominate real estate developer Charles Kushner, father of Trump’s son-in-law Jared Kushner, to serve as ambassador to France.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ohio State, Michigan players involved in melee after Buckeyes loss, multiple people pepper sprayed
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (WCMH) — Michigan players planted their team flag at midfield of Ohio Stadium on Saturday afternoon after a 13-10 win over No. 2 Ohio State, leading to a brawl between players from both teams with police attempting to separate the sides and pepper spray being used.
item.dc:creator: Orri Benatar, WGN 9

item.title: Hidden gems of early Cyber Monday deals
item.description: BestReviews has assembled a collection of top-quality gifts priced under $50, currently available at discounts of up to 60% off.
item.dc:creator: Lizzy Briskin, ABC 4

item.title: Missing hiker survived 50 days in Canadian wilderness, at times in frigid temps
item.description: A 20-year-old hiker who had been missing for weeks was found alive in the wilderness of British Columbia, the Royal Canadian Mountain Police confirmed on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, WGN 9

item.title: Credit card debt is soaring – what to know about getting out of it
item.description: With credit card debt reaching an all-time high, now is the time for the most pertinent questions and answers surrounding a difficult reality many Americans are facing.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump tariffs threaten to crack open North American economies
item.description: MEXICO CITY — President-elect Trump’s pledge to impose tariffs on Mexico and Canada threatens to upend decades of North American integration, a bumpy process that’s recast the two U.S. neighbors as providers of raw and finished goods for the world’s biggest economy.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cucumbers sold in Utah, 25 other states recalled over Salmonella concerns
item.description: A produce company based in Arizona is recalling whole cucumbers that were distributed in more than two dozen states and Canada due to Salmonella concerns.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, ABC 4

item.title: How to avoid starting 'phantom traffic jams' this holiday travel season
item.description: Bumper-to-bumper traffic is a regular occurrence for holiday travelers. However, researchers with Vanderbilt University hope findings from a large traffic study can help.
item.dc:creator: Kori Johnson, ABC 4
